PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexandra Dove in Propria Persona.
Mrs. Dove's Husband was also present.

The Court stated that it is always concerned when someone wants to administer an estate without an Attorney, that the probate code is driven by a lot of rules and requirements that need to be closely followed and complied with, that failure to administer an estate properly can create delays and torpedo an estate, and asked Ms. Dove what she thinks the next step is. Ms. Dove stated that she needs to get letters and go to Vanguard to manage the account, that she needs to pay any outstanding debts and that she is uncertain as to what the next steps will be. Ms. Dove also stated that she would like to avoid getting an Attorney, that she is the only surviving heir, that this is a fairly simple trust estate, that she worked with an Attorney who filed heggstad, that when it was denied he recommended that she could do it on her own and that the Attorney looked over her papers. In addition, Ms. Dove stated that her Husband is helping her, that she has the Nolo Press book, that she understands probate is complicated and that she feels confident that she will be able to go through the steps based on her background and level of education. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for December 31, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for August 31, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
